A node is 'good' if its value is >= all values on the path from root to it. This means its value must be >= the maximum value seen so far on the path.
Use DFS and pass the current maximum (max so far from root to current node) down recursively.
At each node, check if the currentNode.val >= maxSoFar. If yes, count it as good.
Update maxSoFar when recursing into children: maxSoFar = max(maxSoFar, currentNode.val).
